resources.string: Tools for this screen live here: Refresh, Sandbox, Help.
Clears the current list. It does not auto-scan (by design). Use scan when you’re ready.
Opens a testing space. Great for checking UI/logic without a real device.
Each row shows a device: name, status, signal, and quick actions.
This shows the current connection state (Disconnected / Connecting / Connected).
Open device options like details, rename, or settings.
Search for nearby compatible devices. Keep the device close and powered on.
Opens Android’s Bluetooth panel. Enable Bluetooth, pair a new device, or manage paired devices. Return here when you’re done.
Replays this guided tour and shows tips specific to this screen. Use it anytime if you get stuck.
The device’s unique Bluetooth address (MAC). Use it to confirm the correct device or for troubleshooting. Matches the address in Settings > Bluetooth.
This is your control hub. From here you can connect a device, pick effects, set a solid color, and adjust brightness.
Shows whether you’re disconnected, connecting, or connected. When connected, you’ll also see the device alias.
Open the device picker to scan and connect. You can manage saved aliases and see signal strength on the next screen.
Browse dynamic animations (waves, twinkles, etc.). Apply instantly to your connected LEDs.
Pick a single color for your LEDs. Great for quick ambient lighting.
Slide to change global LED brightness. Changes apply live when a device is connected.
Quick navigation: Home, Effects, Solid Color, and Settings.
You’re here. The dashboard shows status, quick actions, and brightness.
Open the effects browser to pick and apply animations.
Choose a single color and apply it instantly.
Theme, haptics, and app info. Reset tutorials or copy debug info from here.
This card shows what your LEDs are currently doing. It updates live whenever the mode changes.
Based on your history, this is the next mode worth trying. Tap it to apply instantly — no need to open Effects or Solid Color first.
Scan for nearby BLE devices, see signal strength, and tap to connect. You can also open device options and rename saved entries.
Pick a single color and apply it to your LEDs. You can fine-tune via picker, HEX, or RGB.
Shows the color you’re about to send. It updates live as you edit.
Drag to choose a hue and shade. Great for quick, visual selection.
Type a color like #8E05C2. Invalid values will prompt a hint so you can fix them.
Adjust Red, Green, and Blue components precisely. Values sync with the picker and HEX.
Send the selected color to the connected device. Works best when a device is connected.
Quick slots for your favorites. Tap to recall; long-press to overwrite with the current color.
Browse animations and set the vibe—waves, pulses, and more. Select to apply instantly.
Scroll through available effects. The focused card is the one you’re selecting.
This overlay highlights the currently centered effect. Scroll to snap; tap to apply.
The device’s display name (or your saved alias). Use it to quickly identify the device in the list.
